chapter_summary: Jesus goes up a mountain and teaches His followers what it truly means to live in God's kingdom. He gives eight blessings for people with humble and pure hearts, tells us we are like salt and light in the world, and teaches us how to treat others with kindness, honesty, and love — even our enemies.
main_lesson: Jesus teaches us that being blessed means having a kind and pure heart, and letting His light shine through everything we do.

memory_verse.kjv: Let your light so shine before men, that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father which is in heaven.
memory_verse.little_bible: Let your light shine bright so everyone can see your good deeds and praise your Father in heaven!

parent_guide: Matthew 5 is the opening of Jesus' Sermon on the Mount — His longest recorded teaching. The Beatitudes (vv1-12) redefine greatness in God's kingdom: not status, wealth, or power, but humility, mercy, purity, and a hunger for righteousness. The salt and light metaphors (vv13-16) call followers to visible, flavourful witness in the world. Jesus then deepens the Law's demands inward: anger is as serious as murder (v22), our words must be simple truth (v37), and love must extend even to enemies (v44). For children, the core message is accessible: God loves kind, honest, humble hearts — and He wants our goodness to shine like a light so others can see Him.
application_for_children: Children can begin practising the Beatitudes by being kind to someone who is sad, making peace with a friend after an argument, or doing a helpful act without being asked. The 'light' metaphor is especially concrete — children understand torches and candles, and can grasp that their good choices make Jesus visible to others.

verses.kjv: And seeing the multitudes, he went up into a mountain: and when he was set, his disciples came unto him:
verses.little_bible: One day Jesus saw a big crowd of people. He walked up a hillside, sat down, and His close friends came and gathered around Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus went up a hill. His friends came to sit with Him.
verses.meaning: Jesus intentionally withdraws to a hillside to teach His disciples, mirroring Moses receiving the Law on Mount Sinai. It signals that something important and authoritative is about to be spoken.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus sat and taught His friends
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for wanting to teach me. Help me listen to Your words every day.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus went up a hill before He started teaching?
verses.family_discussion: Moses received God's law on a mountain. What might Matthew want us to understand by showing Jesus also teaching on a mountain?
verses.do_it_today: Find a quiet spot to sit and read one verse from your Bible together as a family.
verses.illustration_prompt: A warm watercolour scene of Jesus seated on a grassy hillside, surrounded by a small group of disciples leaning in close to listen. Soft afternoon light, rolling hills in the background, gentle expressions of curiosity on the disciples' faces.

verses.kjv: And he opened his mouth, and taught them, saying,
verses.little_bible: Jesus opened His mouth and began to teach them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus spoke. He had important words to share.
verses.meaning: The phrase 'opened his mouth' in Hebrew culture signalled a solemn, significant pronouncement. Jesus is about to deliver words of lasting authority.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus opened His mouth and taught
verses.prayer: God, open my ears and my heart so I can really hear what Jesus says.
verses.discussion_question: What is the most important thing someone has ever taught you?
verses.family_discussion: How do we make sure our family is truly listening when we read the Bible together, not just hearing words?
verses.do_it_today: Before you read the Bible tonight, take one deep breath and say, 'God, I'm listening.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A close watercolour portrait of Jesus beginning to speak, His mouth open gently, eyes warm and kind, disciples leaning forward with attentive faces. Soft golden light.

verses.kjv: Blessed are the poor in spirit: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Happy and truly blessed are the people who know they need God. Heaven's kingdom belongs to them!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: People who know they need God are truly happy. Heaven is theirs!
verses.meaning: 'Poor in spirit' means humbly recognising our deep need for God rather than trusting in our own goodness. Jesus says this posture of humility is the very doorway into His kingdom.
verses.memory_phrase: Humble hearts belong to heaven
verses.prayer: God, I know I need You every day. Thank You that Your kingdom is for people just like me.
verses.discussion_question: What does it feel like to really need someone's help?
verses.family_discussion: The world says confident, self-sufficient people are winners. How does Jesus turn that upside down here?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone in your family one thing you need help with today — practise being humble.
verses.illustration_prompt: A small child looking up with open, empty hands, a soft glow of heavenly light resting on them. Warm watercolour tones. Expression of peaceful trust rather than sadness.

verses.kjv: Blessed are they that mourn: for they shall be comforted.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Blessed are those who are sad and cry. God will come and comfort them and make them feel better.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: When you feel sad, God comes close. He will comfort you.
verses.meaning: Mourning here includes grieving over sin and the brokenness of the world. Jesus promises that those who weep honestly — rather than hiding pain — will experience God's deep, personal comfort.
verses.memory_phrase: God comforts every sad heart
verses.prayer: Jesus, when I feel sad or scared, please come and hold me close. Thank You that You care.
verses.discussion_question: What do you do when a friend is feeling really sad?
verses.family_discussion: How can we as a family make space for honest sadness, trusting God's comfort rather than rushing past hard feelings?
verses.do_it_today: Give someone who seems sad a hug or a kind note today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child sitting quietly with tears on their cheeks, and a gentle warm light wrapping around them like a soft embrace. Watercolour, muted warm tones, sense of peace arriving.

verses.kjv: Blessed are the meek: for they shall inherit the earth.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Blessed are the gentle and humble people. One day they will receive everything God has made!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Gentle, kind people are blessed. They will receive God's world.
verses.meaning: Meekness is not weakness — it is strength held gently under God's control. Jesus echoes Psalm 37 in promising that the meek, not the powerful and aggressive, will ultimately receive the earth as their inheritance.
verses.memory_phrase: Gentle hearts shall inherit the earth
verses.prayer: God, help me be gentle and kind even when I want to be bossy. I trust You to take care of me.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of someone who is gentle and kind? What do you like about them?
verses.family_discussion: In what situations is it hardest for our family to respond with gentleness rather than force? What would it look like to practise meekness there?
verses.do_it_today: When something doesn't go your way today, try responding gently instead of getting upset.
verses.illustration_prompt: A gentle child kneeling to help a smaller child pick up dropped items, surrounded by a lush garden. Soft light, warm watercolour, peaceful and unhurried atmosphere.

verses.kjv: Blessed are they which do hunger and thirst after righteousness: for they shall be filled.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Blessed are the people who really, really want to do what is right and good — as much as a hungry person wants food. God will fill them up!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Want to do right like you want food when hungry. God fills you up!
verses.meaning: Hunger and thirst are urgent, life-or-death cravings. Jesus says people who long for God's righteousness with that same intensity will be completely satisfied — filled by God Himself.
verses.memory_phrase: Long for what is right — be filled
verses.prayer: God, make me want to do what is right as much as I want my favourite food. Fill me with Your goodness.
verses.discussion_question: What does it feel like when you are really, really hungry? How could we want to do right that much?
verses.family_discussion: How do we cultivate a genuine appetite for righteousness in our home — not just rule-following, but truly longing for what is good?
verses.do_it_today: Choose to do one right and kind thing today that nobody asked you to do.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child reaching eagerly toward a glowing piece of bread that transforms into golden light. Expression of joy and longing. Warm watercolour, bright warm tones.

verses.kjv: Blessed are the merciful: for they shall obtain mercy.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Blessed are the people who show kindness and forgiveness to others. God will show that same kindness right back to them!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Be kind and forgiving. God will be kind and forgiving to you too.
verses.meaning: Mercy is actively choosing to help or forgive someone who does not deserve it. Jesus teaches a beautiful reciprocity: as we extend mercy to others, we ourselves experience God's mercy.
verses.memory_phrase: Show mercy — receive mercy
verses.prayer: Jesus, You have been so merciful to me. Help me show that same kindness to others.
verses.discussion_question: Has someone ever forgiven you when you did something wrong? How did it make you feel?
verses.family_discussion: Mercy and justice can feel like opposites. How did Jesus hold them together, and how can we do the same in our family?
verses.do_it_today: Forgive someone today — even if they haven't said sorry — and tell God about it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two children facing each other; one child reaches out a hand in forgiveness, the other takes it with a relieved smile. Soft warm light surrounds them. Watercolour, gentle tones.

verses.kjv: Blessed are the pure in heart: for they shall see God.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Blessed are the people whose hearts are clean and honest inside. They will get to see God!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Keep your heart clean and honest. You will see God!
verses.meaning: Purity of heart means undivided loyalty to God — not performing goodness on the outside while harbouring wrong motives inside. The reward is the greatest imaginable: to see God face to face.
verses.memory_phrase: Pure heart — see God
verses.prayer: God, make my heart clean and honest. I don't want to hide anything from You.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between doing something kind to show off and doing it because you truly care?
verses.family_discussion: How do we help our children develop internal motivation for goodness rather than performing for praise? What does 'heart purity' look like day to day?
verses.do_it_today: Do one kind thing today that nobody sees — keep it just between you and God.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child looking into a clear still pond and seeing a radiant, gentle light reflected back — representing God's presence. Watercolour, blues and golds, sense of wonder.

verses.kjv: Blessed are the peacemakers: for they shall be called the children of God.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Blessed are the people who help others stop fighting and make peace. God will call them His very own children!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Help people stop fighting. God calls you His child!
verses.meaning: Peacemakers do not simply avoid conflict — they actively work to bring reconciliation and healing. This reflects God's own character, and so those who do it bear His family resemblance.
verses.memory_phrase: Peacemakers are God's children
verses.prayer: God, help me be a peacemaker. When friends argue, help me know how to bring kindness and calm.
verses.discussion_question: What could you do if two of your friends were arguing?
verses.family_discussion: What does peacemaking look like in our home? How is it different from just keeping the peace by going silent or giving in?
verses.do_it_today: If anyone is in a disagreement around you today, try to say one kind word that helps them.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child standing between two other children who look upset, gently placing a hand on each of their shoulders. All three begin to smile. Soft watercolour, warm afternoon light.

verses.kjv: Blessed are they which are persecuted for righteousness' sake: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Blessed are people who are treated badly because they do what is right. Heaven's kingdom belongs to them!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: When people are unkind because you do right, heaven is yours!
verses.meaning: Persecution for righteousness means suffering because of one's allegiance to God and His ways. Jesus assures His followers that this costly faithfulness is not lost on God — the kingdom itself is their reward.
verses.memory_phrase: Stand for right — heaven is yours
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me keep doing what is right even when it is hard. Thank You that heaven belongs to people who trust You.
verses.discussion_question: Has anyone ever made fun of you for doing the right thing? What did you do?
verses.family_discussion: How do we prepare our children for the reality that faithfulness to Jesus can sometimes be costly, while still giving them courage and hope?
verses.do_it_today: Choose to do the right thing today even if you think someone might laugh at you for it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child standing straight and calm while other children around them point and laugh. A soft golden crown of light hovers gently above the child's head. Watercolour, warm and reassuring tones.

verses.kjv: Blessed are ye, when men shall revile you, and persecute you, and shall say all manner of evil against you falsely, for my sake.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'You are blessed when people say mean and untrue things about you because you love and follow Me.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: When people say mean, untrue things about you for loving Jesus, you are blessed.
verses.meaning: Jesus becomes personal here — 'for my sake' links the disciples' future suffering directly to their loyalty to Him. Being mistreated for Jesus' name is counted as honour.
verses.memory_phrase: Blessed when mocked for Jesus' name
verses.prayer: Jesus, if anyone is ever unkind to me because I love You, help me remember I am blessed and You are with me.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think some people might not like it when others talk about Jesus?
verses.family_discussion: How do we model responding to criticism or mockery with grace rather than bitterness, so our children can do the same?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone you trust one way you have felt brave about loving Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child wearing a cross necklace, surrounded by a small group of children who look skeptical, but the child smiles peacefully. Soft warm light around the child. Watercolour, gentle tones.

verses.kjv: Rejoice, and be exceeding glad: for great is your reward in heaven: for so persecuted they the prophets which were before you.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Be happy and jump for joy! Your reward in heaven is great. Remember, people were also unkind to God's prophets long ago, just like you.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Be very glad! Your reward in heaven is great.
verses.meaning: Rather than reacting to mistreatment with despair or anger, Jesus calls His followers to genuine celebration rooted in the certainty of their heavenly reward. They join a long and honoured line of God's faithful people.
verses.memory_phrase: Great is your reward in heaven
verses.prayer: God, thank You that my reward in heaven is great. Help me be joyful even when things are hard.
verses.discussion_question: What is the best reward you can imagine? How is heaven even better?
verses.family_discussion: How does a long-term heavenly perspective help us keep our hearts joyful when things are unfair right now?
verses.do_it_today: Write or draw a picture of something you are looking forward to in heaven.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child jumping joyfully, arms wide, golden light streaming down from above. Bright, cheerful watercolour. Expression of pure delight and hope.

verses.kjv: Ye are the salt of the earth: but if the salt have lost his savour, wherewith shall it be salted? it is thenceforth good for nothing, but to be cast out, and to be trodden under foot of men.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'You are like salt in the world! Salt makes food taste better and keeps it from going bad. But if salt stops being salty, it is no good anymore. So keep being the good and flavourful person God made you to be!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: You are like salt! Salt makes things good. Stay good like salt.
verses.meaning: Salt preserves, flavours, and purifies. Jesus says His followers are meant to have that same preserving, enhancing effect on the world around them. But a disciple who loses their distinctiveness becomes useless.
verses.memory_phrase: You are the salt of the earth
verses.prayer: God, help me make the world around me a little better — like salt makes food taste good.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever tasted food without any salt? What was it like? How can you be 'salt' at school or home?
verses.family_discussion: In what practical ways does our family function as 'salt' in our neighbourhood and community?
verses.do_it_today: Do one small thing today to make someone's day a little better — be salt!
verses.illustration_prompt: A child happily sprinkling salt onto a steaming bowl of food at a family table. Warm kitchen scene, soft light, joyful expressions. Watercolour, rich warm tones.

verses.kjv: Ye are the light of the world. A city that is set on an hill cannot be hid.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'You are the light of the whole world! Just like a city built on a hilltop shines at night and everyone can see it — you can't be hidden.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: You are the light of the world! A bright city on a hill can be seen by all.
verses.meaning: Jesus declares that His followers, filled with His light, are meant to be publicly visible. Goodness lived out in the open cannot be concealed — it shines out like a city on a hill.
verses.memory_phrase: You are the light of the world
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You for putting Your light in me. Help me shine it brightly so others can see You.
verses.discussion_question: What is the brightest light you have ever seen? How do you think you can be a light for people around you?
verses.family_discussion: What does it look like for our family to be a 'city on a hill' in our street, school, and community?
verses.do_it_today: Do something kind and visible today — let your light shine where others can see it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A hillside at dusk with a glowing town of warm lights. In the foreground a child looks up at it with wonder. Watercolour, blues, purples, and golden lights. Magical and warm.

verses.kjv: Neither do men light a candle, and put it under a bushel, but on a candlestick; and it giveth light unto all that are in the house.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Nobody lights a candle and then covers it up with a bowl! They put it on a stand so it lights up the whole room for everyone.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: No one hides a lit candle. They put it high so it lights the whole room.
verses.meaning: The point of light is illumination — hiding it defeats its purpose entirely. Jesus says His followers are meant to be placed prominently and shine out, not hidden away.
verses.memory_phrase: Don't hide your candle — let it shine
verses.prayer: God, I don't want to hide the good You put in me. Help me shine brightly for You.
verses.discussion_question: Why would it be silly to cover a candle with a bowl? What would happen?
verses.family_discussion: Are there areas where we as a family hide our faith or our goodness? How could we share it more openly?
verses.do_it_today: Share something good about your faith with someone today — don't hide your light.
verses.illustration_prompt: A glowing candle on a tall candlestick in the centre of a warm room, casting light onto all the happy faces of a family gathered around. Watercolour, golden warm tones.

verses.kjv: Let your light so shine before men, that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father which is in heaven.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Let your light shine brightly for everyone to see! When you do good things, people will see it and praise your Father in heaven.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Shine your light so all can see your good deeds and praise God!
verses.meaning: The goal of visible goodness is not personal glory but that others will see God through us and praise Him. Our deeds point like arrows beyond ourselves to our Father.
verses.memory_phrase: Let your light shine for God's glory
verses.prayer: God, help everything I do today point people toward You. I want my life to bring You glory.
verses.discussion_question: When you do something kind, how do you think it can help someone learn about God?
verses.family_discussion: How do we teach children to live visibly for God without becoming proud or showy? What is the difference?
verses.do_it_today: Shine your light by helping someone today — then thank God for letting you be His light.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child holding up a glowing lantern at night, warm light spilling onto the faces of people nearby who look upward with expressions of wonder and gratitude. Watercolour, deep blues and warm gold.

verses.kjv: Think not that I am come to destroy the law, or the prophets: I am not come to destroy, but to fulfil.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Don't think I came to get rid of God's laws and the words of the prophets. No — I came to complete them and show what they truly mean!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus did not come to remove God's rules. He came to complete them.
verses.meaning: Jesus positions Himself not as an opponent of the Old Testament but as its perfect fulfilment. Every law and prophecy finds its true meaning in Him.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus came to fulfil the Law
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You came to complete everything God promised. Help me follow You.
verses.discussion_question: If your teacher gave you a puzzle to finish, what would it mean to 'complete' it?
verses.family_discussion: How does understanding that Jesus fulfils the Law (rather than abolishing it) shape how we read the Old Testament with our children?
verses.do_it_today: Read one Old Testament promise and look for how Jesus fulfilled it.
verses.illustration_prompt: An open scroll of ancient text with golden light emanating from it, leading upward toward a figure of Jesus standing with arms open. Watercolour, warm sepia and gold tones.

verses.kjv: For verily I say unto you, Till heaven and earth pass, one jot or one tittle shall in no wise pass from the law, till all be fulfilled.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'I promise you — not even the tiniest letter of God's Word will disappear until everything has been completed. God's Word is completely true!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Every small part of God's Word is true and will not be lost.
verses.meaning: A 'jot' and 'tittle' are the smallest marks in Hebrew writing. Jesus is emphasising the complete reliability and permanence of God's Word down to its finest detail.
verses.memory_phrase: God's Word will never pass away
verses.prayer: God, thank You that Your Word is completely true — every single word. Help me trust it.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever made a very serious promise? How did you feel about keeping it?
verses.family_discussion: What does the permanence and trustworthiness of Scripture mean for how we approach it day to day as a family?
verses.do_it_today: Find a favourite Bible verse and write it somewhere you will see it — a reminder that God's Word lasts.
verses.illustration_prompt: A beautifully illuminated ancient Bible open on a table, tiny golden letters glowing, surrounded by soft light. Watercolour, rich warm tones, sense of reverence.

verses.kjv: Whosoever therefore shall break one of these least commandments, and shall teach men so, he shall be called the least in the kingdom of heaven: but whosoever shall do and teach them, the same shall be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Anyone who breaks even the smallest of God's rules and teaches others to do the same will be the least important in heaven. But anyone who obeys and teaches others to obey will be truly great in heaven!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Obey God's rules and help others obey. That makes you great in God's kingdom.
verses.meaning: Jesus places great weight on both practising and teaching God's commands. Greatness in His kingdom is not fame or power but faithful obedience and the encouragement of others to do likewise.
verses.memory_phrase: Obey and teach — be great in God's kingdom
verses.prayer: God, help me obey You and help others to obey You too. I want to be faithful.
verses.discussion_question: If you learned something really important, would you want to share it with your friends? Why?
verses.family_discussion: How are we as parents living as examples of obedience to God — not just telling our children what to do but showing them how?
verses.do_it_today: Teach a sibling, friend, or family member one thing from the Bible that you have learned.
verses.illustration_prompt: An older child sitting with a younger child, an open Bible between them, the older child pointing to a verse and explaining it gently. Warm afternoon light. Watercolour, soft tones.

verses.kjv: For I say unto you, That except your righteousness shall exceed the righteousness of the scribes and Pharisees, ye shall in no case enter into the kingdom of heaven.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'I tell you the truth — unless your goodness goes much deeper than just following rules on the outside, you will not enter God's kingdom.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: True goodness comes from inside your heart, not just following outside rules.
verses.meaning: The Pharisees were meticulous about outward rule-keeping but neglected heart transformation. Jesus calls His followers to a deeper, inward righteousness — one that flows from love for God rather than desire for approval.
verses.memory_phrase: True righteousness starts in the heart
verses.prayer: Jesus, I don't want to just look good on the outside. Change my heart to truly love what is right.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between doing the right thing because you truly want to and doing it just so people see you?
verses.family_discussion: What are the 'Pharisee habits' our family might fall into — performing good behaviour outwardly without heart engagement? How do we guard against that?
verses.do_it_today: Ask yourself: am I being kind today because I truly care, or because I want someone to notice? Try to do one truly kind thing from the heart.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two figures side by side: one dressed in fancy religious robes looking proud, the other a plain child with hands folded and eyes closed in sincere prayer, with a warm glow around their heart. Watercolour.

verses.kjv: Ye have heard that it was said by them of old time, Thou shalt not kill; and whosoever shall kill shall be in danger of the judgment:
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'You have heard the old law that says, Do not hurt or kill anyone. And people who do that will be judged.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: You have heard: do not hurt or kill. Those who do will face judgment.
verses.meaning: Jesus begins a series of contrasts between the traditional understanding of the Law and His deeper interpretation. The prohibition against murder is widely known — but Jesus is about to take it deeper.
verses.memory_phrase: Do not harm one another
verses.prayer: God, help me never hurt anyone with my hands, my words, or my heart.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think God cares so much about people being safe and not being hurt?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus is about to expand this commandment far beyond physical violence. How do we help children understand that harm begins long before actions?
verses.do_it_today: Choose to be gentle with your hands and your words to everyone you meet today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child holding their hands gently, open and upward, as if offering peace. Soft watercolour, calm expression, warm light.

verses.kjv: But I say unto you, That whosoever is angry with his brother without a cause shall be in danger of the judgment: and whosoever shall say to his brother, Raca, shall be in danger of the council: but whosoever shall say, Thou fool, shall be in danger of hell fire.
verses.little_bible: But Jesus said, 'I tell you something deeper — even being angry at someone for no good reason is wrong. And calling people horrible names is very serious. Words can hurt just like actions can.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Angry words that hurt people are wrong too. Be kind with your words.
verses.meaning: Jesus traces violence back to its root: uncontrolled anger and contemptuous speech. The heart that harbours rage or uses words to destroy others is already in dangerous territory before any physical action occurs.
verses.memory_phrase: Angry words hurt — speak kindly
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me control my anger and never use my words to hurt someone. Tame my heart and my tongue.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever said something that hurt someone's feelings? How did it make you feel after?
verses.family_discussion: How does our family handle anger? What habits help us express frustration without harming one another with words?
verses.do_it_today: Before you say something when you are frustrated today, take a breath and choose your words carefully.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child with a raised fist, pausing as a soft glowing hand appears to gently lower theirs, expression changing from angry to calm. Watercolour, warm gentle tones.

verses.kjv: Therefore if thou bring thy gift to the altar, and there rememberest that thy brother hath ought against thee;
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Imagine you are bringing a gift to God, and then you suddenly remember that a friend or family member is upset with you...'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: If you are giving God a gift and remember someone is upset with you — stop.
verses.meaning: Jesus interrupts the act of worship to address the priority of human relationships. Unresolved conflict with others affects our relationship with God and must be addressed before worship.
verses.memory_phrase: Make peace before you worship
verses.prayer: God, help me not ignore things that are wrong between me and others. Remind me to make peace.
verses.discussion_question: What does it feel like to try to be happy and thankful when you are in the middle of an argument with someone?
verses.family_discussion: What would it look like for our family to practise resolving conflict before gathering for prayer or church?
verses.do_it_today: Think of someone you have a disagreement with. Plan how you will go and sort it out.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child kneeling with a small gift in their hands, a thoughtful and slightly worried expression on their face, as they remember something important. Warm light, watercolour, soft tones.

verses.kjv: Leave there thy gift before the altar, and go thy way; first be reconciled to thy brother, and then come and offer thy gift.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Leave your gift right there in front of God. Go first and make things right with the person who is upset. Then come back and give your gift to God.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: First go make peace with your friend. Then come back to give God your gift.
verses.meaning: Reconciliation with others is so important to God that He asks us to pause even our worship to pursue it. Restored relationships are themselves an act of worship.
verses.memory_phrase: First make peace — then worship
verses.prayer: God, help me never let an argument sit. Give me courage to go and make things right.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think God cares so much about us being friends again before we talk to Him?
verses.family_discussion: Reconciliation takes courage. How do we build that courage in our children — modelling apology and forgiveness ourselves?
verses.do_it_today: Go and say sorry to someone, or forgive someone today before your bedtime prayer.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two children facing each other with small smiles, one reaching out a hand to the other in reconciliation. Warm afternoon light, joyful watercolour tones.

verses.kjv: Agree with thine adversary quickly, whiles thou art in the way with him; lest at any time the adversary deliver thee to the judge, and the judge deliver thee to the officer, and thou be cast into prison.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'If someone has a problem with you, sort it out quickly! If you don't, it could get much worse — like getting in trouble with a judge.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Solve problems with others quickly before they get much worse.
verses.meaning: Jesus urges prompt resolution of conflicts. Delay in addressing grievances escalates situations beyond our control. There is wisdom and safety in seeking peace quickly.
verses.memory_phrase: Settle things quickly — don't delay
verses.prayer: God, help me not put off sorting out problems with people. Give me courage to act quickly.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever had a small problem that got bigger because nobody talked about it? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: What patterns help our family address small conflicts before they grow into larger ones?
verses.do_it_today: If there is something unresolved with someone in your life, take the first small step to sort it out today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child and another child walking side by side on a path, talking and working things out, before they arrive at a gate together in harmony. Watercolour, warm path-light, peaceful tones.

verses.kjv: Verily I say unto thee, Thou shalt by no means come out thence, till thou hast paid the uttermost farthing.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'I promise you — if you end up before a judge, you won't get out until you have paid every last penny. So make things right before it comes to that!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Don't wait — make things right now before it is too late.
verses.meaning: Jesus emphasises the full consequence of unresolved conflict. The image of the debtor's prison underlines that ignoring problems does not make them go away — urgency in peacemaking is essential.
verses.memory_phrase: Make things right before it is too late
verses.prayer: God, I don't want to wait too long to fix things. Help me be brave and act now.
verses.discussion_question: If you owe a friend something or need to say sorry, what happens if you just keep waiting?
verses.family_discussion: What accountability structures in our family encourage us to deal with conflict promptly rather than letting it fester?
verses.do_it_today: Write a note or think of something kind to do for someone you have hurt or ignored.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child standing at a doorway looking concerned, holding a small pouch — a picture of urgency and responsibility. Watercolour, muted but warm, sense of gentle urgency.

verses.kjv: Ye have heard that it was said by them of old time, Thou shalt not commit adultery:
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'You have heard the old rule that says we must be faithful and true to the people we love and care for.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: You have heard: be faithful and true to the people you love.
verses.meaning: Jesus introduces the commandment against adultery as a lead-in to His deeper teaching that faithfulness and purity begin in the heart, not just in outward actions.
verses.memory_phrase: Be faithful and true
verses.prayer: God, help me be faithful and true — in my heart and in my actions.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to be truly loyal to someone you love?
verses.family_discussion: How do we build a culture of faithfulness and trustworthiness in our home — keeping our promises and honouring one another?
verses.do_it_today: Keep every promise you make today — even a small one.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two hands clasped together in friendship and faithfulness against a warm golden background. Watercolour, simple and warm, sense of trust.

verses.kjv: But I say unto you, That whosoever looketh on a woman to lust after her hath committed adultery with her already in his heart.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'But I tell you something deeper — faithfulness starts inside your heart. God wants our thoughts and our eyes to be pure and honouring to others too.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God wants our hearts and eyes to be pure and honouring to others.
verses.meaning: Jesus traces unfaithfulness to its root in the heart and the gaze. Purity is not only about actions — it begins with guarding the mind and eyes and choosing to honour others in our thoughts.
verses.memory_phrase: Keep your heart and eyes pure
verses.prayer: God, help me keep my heart and eyes pure. Help me honour and respect all people.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think God cares about what we think about, not just what we do?
verses.family_discussion: How do we practically help our children develop habits of guarding their eyes and thoughts in an age full of screens and images?
verses.do_it_today: Choose to look away from something unkind or wrong today — honour God with your eyes.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child gently turning their face away from a distracting screen, choosing instead to look at a beautiful garden through a window. Watercolour, warm and peaceful tones.

verses.kjv: And if thy right eye offend thee, pluck it out, and cast it from thee: for it is profitable for thee that one of thy members should perish, and not that thy whole body should be cast into hell.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'If something is leading you to do wrong, deal with it firmly! It is better to remove a small thing from your life that causes harm than to let it pull your whole life in the wrong direction.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Remove what leads you the wrong way. It is better to lose one thing than everything.
verses.meaning: Jesus uses vivid hyperbole to emphasise that sin must be dealt with radically. No comfort or convenience is worth allowing something to pull us away from God. Decisive action against temptation is necessary.
verses.memory_phrase: Remove what pulls you away from God
verses.prayer: God, help me be brave enough to stop doing things that pull me away from You.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of something that sometimes makes you want to do the wrong thing? What could you do about it?
verses.family_discussion: What does 'radical dealing with temptation' look like in our family's media use, friendships, and daily habits?
verses.do_it_today: Remove one thing from today's routine that pulls you toward bad choices — a game, a show, or a habit.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child confidently putting down a tempting but harmful object and walking away toward bright open light. Watercolour, warm tones, sense of freedom.

verses.kjv: And if thy right hand offend thee, cut it off, and cast it from thee: for it is profitable for thee that one of thy members should perish, and not that thy whole body should be cast into hell.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'The same is true for anything you do with your hands — if your actions are pulling you away from God, stop and change what you do! Nothing is worth losing God.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Stop doing things that lead you away from God. God is worth more than anything.
verses.meaning: Continuing the hyperbole from verse 29, Jesus extends the principle to actions and habits. The call is to deal decisively with sinful behaviours — no matter how natural or comfortable they feel — before they lead to greater destruction.
verses.memory_phrase: Nothing is worth losing God
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me stop doing things that take me away from You. You are worth everything.
verses.discussion_question: If something you were doing was hurting your friendship with God, what would you do?
verses.family_discussion: How do we help children develop the habit of self-examination — regularly asking, 'Are my habits drawing me toward God or away from Him?'
verses.do_it_today: Ask yourself: what am I doing today that draws me closer to God? Do more of that.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child opening their hands wide and releasing something that turns to smoke, then raising their open hands upward toward warm light. Watercolour, sense of release and freedom.

verses.kjv: It hath been said, Whosoever shall put away his wife, let him give her a writing of divorcement:
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'You have heard the old rule about the paperwork people give when they break up a family.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: You have heard the old rule about breaking up a family.
verses.meaning: Jesus acknowledges the existing practice of divorce under Mosaic Law but is about to challenge its casual use. God's design is covenant faithfulness, not convenient exit.
verses.memory_phrase: God cares about family promises
verses.prayer: God, thank You that You care about families. Help families stay together and love each other well.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think God cares about families staying together and being happy?
verses.family_discussion: How do we talk with children about the serious nature of family promises while offering compassion for the pain when families do break apart?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone in your family one reason you love having them as part of your family.
verses.illustration_prompt: A warm family scene — a mother, father, and children holding hands together under a big tree. Soft watercolour, golden afternoon light, expressions of belonging and love.

verses.kjv: But I say unto you, That whosoever shall put away his wife, saving for the cause of fornication, causeth her to commit adultery: and whosoever shall marry her that is divorced committeth adultery.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'But I tell you — God takes the promises families make very seriously. Breaking those promises without a serious reason causes real hurt and harm to everyone.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God takes family promises very seriously. Broken promises cause hurt.
verses.meaning: Jesus elevates the sanctity of marriage beyond legal technicality to covenant seriousness. God designed marriage as a permanent bond, and breaking it lightly has deep and rippling consequences.
verses.memory_phrase: God takes our promises seriously
verses.prayer: God, help me take my promises seriously, just like You do. Help me be trustworthy.
verses.discussion_question: What happens when someone breaks an important promise? How does it make the other person feel?
verses.family_discussion: How do we model the weight and joy of covenant commitment in our family — showing children that promises matter and are worth keeping?
verses.do_it_today: Make one promise today and keep it, no matter what.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two hands holding a single golden ring between them, light shining around the ring, a warm glow. Watercolour, sacred and tender feeling.

verses.kjv: Again, ye have heard that it hath been said by them of old time, Thou shalt not forswear thyself, but shalt perform unto the Lord thine oaths:
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'You have also heard the old teaching — don't break your promises, and always do what you swear to God you will do.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: You have heard: don't break promises made to God.
verses.meaning: The Law required that oaths made to God be kept. Jesus is going to challenge the entire system of oath-swearing and call for a simpler, more radical honesty.
verses.memory_phrase: Keep every promise you make
verses.prayer: God, help me be someone who always keeps their word.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to make a really serious promise? When have you kept a hard promise?
verses.family_discussion: How do we cultivate in our children a deep respect for the spoken word — understanding that what they say should be reliable?
verses.do_it_today: Think of a promise you have made and check: have you kept it? If not, go do it now.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child with one hand over their heart and one raised, making a sincere promise. Expression of earnest commitment. Watercolour, warm confident tones.

verses.kjv: But I say unto you, Swear not at all; neither by heaven; for it is God's throne:
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'But I tell you — don't swear oaths at all! Not by heaven, because heaven is where God sits on His throne.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus says: don't make oaths at all. Just tell the truth!
verses.meaning: Jesus calls His followers to such complete honesty that they need no oaths to back up their words. A disciple's simple word should be completely trustworthy.
verses.memory_phrase: Be so honest you need no oath
verses.prayer: God, help me be so trustworthy that my 'yes' always means yes and my 'no' always means no.
verses.discussion_question: If you always told the truth, would anyone need to ask you to promise?
verses.family_discussion: What does radical honesty look like as a family value — where everyone trusts that each person's plain word is reliable?
verses.do_it_today: Practise only saying things you truly mean today. Let your word be enough.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child speaking with a clear, open expression — simple, direct, trustworthy. A small beam of golden light comes from their mouth as they speak. Watercolour, clean warm tones.

verses.kjv: Nor by the earth; for it is his footstool: neither by Jerusalem; for it is the city of the great King.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Don't swear by the earth either — it's God's footstool! And not by Jerusalem — that is the city of the Great King!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The earth and Jerusalem belong to God. Don't use them to make oaths.
verses.meaning: Everything in creation belongs to God. No thing or place can be used as a truly independent guarantee of our words — all are under God's ownership and authority.
verses.memory_phrase: All creation belongs to God
verses.prayer: God, everything belongs to You — the sky, the earth, the cities. You are so great!
verses.discussion_question: Can you name some amazing things that belong to God? How does that make you feel?
verses.family_discussion: How does a sense of God's ownership of everything shape how we live, speak, and hold possessions as a family?
verses.do_it_today: Look around outside and name five things that belong to God. Praise Him for each one.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child standing outdoors looking up at a vast sky and wide earth, arms stretched wide in wonder. Watercolour, sweeping wide landscape, sense of awe and smallness before God's greatness.

verses.kjv: Neither shalt thou swear by thy head, because thou canst not make one hair white or black.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Don't even swear by your own head! You can't even change one hair on your head from white to black — you're not in control of everything!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: You can't even change one hair's colour. You're not in control — so just tell the truth!
verses.meaning: Even our own bodies are not fully in our control — they belong to God. Using ourselves as a guarantee of our words is therefore meaningless. Simple truthfulness is the only solid foundation.
verses.memory_phrase: God is in control — just tell the truth
verses.prayer: God, I know I am not in control of everything. Help me be honest and trust You.
verses.discussion_question: What are some things that only God can control? How does that make you trust Him more?
verses.family_discussion: How does acknowledging our limits and God's sovereignty actually free us to live more honestly and humbly?
verses.do_it_today: Make a list of three things only God can control. Thank Him for each one.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child looking at their own reflection in a pond, laughing as they try to change their hair colour with their fingers — and failing! Playful watercolour scene, warm and funny tones.

verses.kjv: But let your communication be, Yea, yea; Nay, nay: for whatsoever is more than these cometh of evil.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'Simply let your yes mean yes and your no mean no. Anything more than that comes from a wrong place.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Say yes when you mean yes. Say no when you mean no. Be simply honest.
verses.meaning: Jesus calls for radical simplicity in speech — no need for elaborate oaths or qualifications. A person of integrity whose word can be trusted needs only a plain 'yes' or 'no'.
verses.memory_phrase: Let your yes mean yes
verses.prayer: Jesus, help my yes always mean yes and my no always mean no. Make me a person others can truly trust.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it important that people can trust what you say?
verses.family_discussion: Are there ways in which our family speech has become complicated or evasive? How can we cultivate a culture of plain, simple, trustworthy honesty?
verses.do_it_today: Practise saying what you mean simply and clearly today — no exaggerating, no excuses.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child looking another person in the eyes with a calm, clear smile, simply nodding their head with an honest expression. Clean, clear watercolour, bright tones.

verses.kjv: Ye have heard that it hath been said, An eye for an eye, and a tooth for a tooth:
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'You have heard the old saying — if someone hurts you, hurt them back the same way.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: You have heard: if someone hurts you, hurt them back.
verses.meaning: The 'eye for an eye' principle was originally designed to limit excessive revenge — an innovation for justice in its day. But Jesus is about to challenge even this, calling His followers to something entirely new.
verses.memory_phrase: Old way: hurt for hurt
verses.prayer: God, help me not want to hurt people back when they hurt me. Teach me a better way.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever wanted to get back at someone who hurt you? How did that feel?
verses.family_discussion: The 'eye for an eye' principle sounds fair — but what does a world full of that look like? What does Jesus offer instead?
verses.do_it_today: If someone does something unkind to you today, try responding with kindness instead.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two children facing each other with crossed arms and upset expressions, but then a gentle teacher figure steps in between them with an open, calming gesture. Watercolour, warm soft light.

verses.kjv: But I say unto you, That ye resist not evil: but whosoever shall smite thee on thy right cheek, turn to him the other also.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'But I tell you something different — don't try to get revenge. If someone is unkind or hurts you, surprise them with kindness instead of fighting back.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Don't fight back. Surprise unkindness with kindness instead.
verses.meaning: Jesus introduces a revolutionary response to mistreatment: non-retaliation and active, dignified non-violence. Turning the other cheek is not weakness — it breaks the cycle of retaliation and demonstrates a higher power.
verses.memory_phrase: Answer unkindness with kindness
verses.prayer: Jesus, when someone is mean to me, help me be kind back. That's hard — I need Your help!
verses.discussion_question: How do you think someone would feel if you were kind to them after they were mean to you?
verses.family_discussion: How do we help our children respond to bullying or unfairness with dignified non-retaliation — without teaching them to simply be passive and allow harm?
verses.do_it_today: The next time something unfair happens to you, try responding with one kind action instead of retaliation.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child who has been pushed over on a playground reaches up and offers a helping hand to the child who pushed them. The other child looks surprised and touched. Warm watercolour, gentle and powerful.

verses.kjv: And if any man will sue thee at the law, and take away thy coat, let him have thy cloke also.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'If someone takes something from you, offer them even more. Be generous instead of grasping.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: If someone takes from you, offer even more. Be generous!
verses.meaning: Jesus calls His followers to radical generosity that disarms conflict. Rather than fighting over possessions, a disciple freely offers more than demanded — demonstrating that things don't have ultimate value.
verses.memory_phrase: Be more generous than expected
verses.prayer: God, help me hold my things loosely. Help me be generous even when it's hard.
verses.discussion_question: What is something you own that you would find it hard to give away? What if God asked you to?
verses.family_discussion: What is our family's posture toward possessions — do we hold them tightly or loosely? How do we model generosity beyond what is required?
verses.do_it_today: Give something away today that you didn't have to — be surprisingly generous.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child handing over their favourite jacket to another child, smiling warmly. The receiving child looks astonished and touched. Watercolour, warm light, joyful generosity.

verses.kjv: And if any man shall compel thee to go a mile, go with him twain.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'If someone forces you to carry something or help them for one mile — go two miles! Do more than they asked.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: If someone needs help for one mile, go two miles! Do more than asked.
verses.meaning: Roman law allowed soldiers to conscript civilians to carry burdens for one mile. Jesus tells His followers to go a second — voluntarily. This unexpected generosity transforms compulsion into a gift and demonstrates kingdom values.
verses.memory_phrase: Go further than asked
verses.prayer: God, help me go beyond what I have to do when helping others. Make me a generous helper.
verses.discussion_question: If a friend needed your help and you helped more than they asked, how do you think they would feel?
verses.family_discussion: How does going 'the extra mile' in our family — doing more than required — reflect God's character and build family culture?
verses.do_it_today: Do one chore or kind act today that nobody asked you to — go the extra mile.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child carrying a heavy bag for an older person on a path, smiling and walking energetically, going further than expected. Watercolour, warm bright tones, sense of joy in service.

verses.kjv: Give to him that asketh thee, and from him that would borrow of thee turn not thou away.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'When someone asks you for something, give to them. Don't turn away from someone who needs help or wants to borrow something.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Give when someone asks. Don't turn away from someone who needs help.
verses.meaning: Jesus calls His followers to open-handed generosity — not turning away those in need but actively giving. This reflects God's own generous character.
verses.memory_phrase: Give freely to those who ask
verses.prayer: God, give me a generous heart. Help me not turn away from people who need help.
verses.discussion_question: How do you feel when someone shares something with you when you really needed it?
verses.family_discussion: How does our family practise generosity — particularly toward those outside our circle of close friends and family?
verses.do_it_today: If anyone asks for your help today, say yes — and give more than they asked for.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child opening their hands wide and giving a piece of fruit to a smaller child who looks hungry. Both are smiling. Soft warm watercolour, generous and joyful atmosphere.

verses.kjv: Ye have heard that it hath been said, Thou shalt love thy neighbour, and hate thine enemy.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'You have heard the old saying — love the people near you and your friends, but it is okay to dislike your enemies.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: You have heard: love your neighbour. Hate your enemy.
verses.meaning: The command to love neighbours came from Leviticus, but the addition of 'hate your enemies' was a popular but not Scriptural extension. Jesus is about to challenge this with a radically different command.
verses.memory_phrase: Old way: love friends, hate enemies
verses.prayer: God, teach me Your way of loving — even the people I find difficult.
verses.discussion_question: Who are the easiest people to be kind to? Who is it hardest to be kind to?
verses.family_discussion: What is the difference between what culture tells us about enemies and what Jesus is about to say? Which version do we practise at home?
verses.do_it_today: Think of someone who is difficult to love. Say a prayer for them today.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two groups of children on different sides of a playground, arms crossed and facing away from each other. Watercolour, slightly muted colours to show separation and distance.

verses.kjv: But I say unto you, Love your enemies, bless them that curse you, do good to them that hate you, and pray for them which despitefully use you, and persecute you;
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'But I tell you — love your enemies! Say kind things about people who are unkind to you. Do good to those who don't like you. Pray for people who treat you badly.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Love your enemies. Be kind to them. Pray for them. That is Jesus' way!
verses.meaning: This is one of the most radical commands in all of Scripture. Enemy-love is not natural — it is supernatural, made possible only by God's love working through us. It breaks cycles of hatred and demonstrates the kingdom.
verses.memory_phrase: Love your enemies — pray for them
verses.prayer: God, help me love the people who are not kind to me. Help me pray for them and be kind back.
verses.discussion_question: Is there anyone who is unkind to you? Can you pray for them right now?
verses.family_discussion: Enemy-love is the signature of God's kingdom. How do we practically model this for our children — in the way we talk about difficult people, neighbours, politicians, and those who have hurt us?
verses.do_it_today: Write down the name of one person who has been unkind to you and say a prayer for them right now.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child kneeling in prayer with their hands folded, a picture of a 'difficult' child beside them as they pray for them. Warm light all around. Watercolour, peaceful and courageous atmosphere.

verses.kjv: That ye may be the children of your Father which is in heaven: for he maketh his sun to rise on the evil and on the good, and sendeth rain on the just and on the unjust.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'When you love like that, you show that you are truly God's children! God is kind to everyone — He makes the sun shine on good people and not-so-good people alike, and He sends rain for everyone.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God shines the sun on everyone — good and bad. Love like your Father does.
verses.meaning: God's common grace extends to all people regardless of their character. His children are called to imitate this unconditional generosity and love — reflecting the Father's character to the world.
verses.memory_phrase: Love like your Father loves — everyone
verses.prayer: God, help me love people the way You do — generously and without leaving anyone out.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever noticed that the sun shines on everyone? What does that tell you about how much God loves people?
verses.family_discussion: How can our family demonstrate God's unconditional love — to those who are different from us, difficult, or even hostile toward us?
verses.do_it_today: Do one kind thing today for someone who doesn't expect your kindness — show them God's love.
verses.illustration_prompt: A bright sun shining down over a wide landscape with all kinds of people — children of different backgrounds playing together under the same warm light. Watercolour, joyful and inclusive.

verses.kjv: For if ye love them which love you, what reward have ye? do not even the publicans the same?
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'If you only love the people who love you back, what is so special about that? Even people who don't follow God do that much!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Loving only people who love you back is easy. Jesus calls us to do more.
verses.meaning: Loving friends is instinctive and universal — there is nothing distinctive about it. The mark of a Kingdom person is love that reaches beyond natural affection to those who cannot or will not return it.
verses.memory_phrase: Love beyond those who love you back
verses.prayer: God, help me love people even when they don't love me back. Help me go beyond easy love.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it easy to love people who are kind to you? Why is it harder to love people who aren't?
verses.family_discussion: How do we stretch our family's circle of love and care — consistently reaching beyond those who naturally return our affection?
verses.do_it_today: Be kind to someone today who isn't usually kind to you — go beyond easy love.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child walking toward a group of children they don't know well, arm extended in a friendly wave. The children look surprised but begin to smile. Watercolour, warm open tones.

verses.kjv: And if ye salute your brethren only, what do ye more than others? do not even the publicans so?
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'And if you are only friendly to your own friends, how are you different from everyone else? Even people who don't know God do that!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Being friendly only to your friends is nothing special. Be friendly to everyone!
verses.meaning: Greeting only insiders is normal human behaviour — there is no distinctiveness in that. Jesus calls His followers to an expansive, outward-reaching welcome that reflects God's own inclusive love.
verses.memory_phrase: Welcome everyone — not just your friends
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me say hello and be kind to everyone — not just my best friends.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever seen someone sitting alone? What could you do?
verses.family_discussion: What practical habits can our family adopt to be more welcoming — to new families in our street, at school, or at church?
verses.do_it_today: Say hello and smile at someone you don't usually talk to today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child walking up to another child sitting alone on a bench and sitting down next to them with a big warm smile. Watercolour, bright warm school-day tones.

verses.kjv: Be ye therefore perfect, even as your Father which is in heaven is perfect.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said, 'So grow up to be whole and complete in love — just like your Father in heaven is perfect and complete in love.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Grow in love to be more and more like your Father in heaven.
verses.meaning: The word 'perfect' here (teleios) means whole, complete, and mature — not sinless perfection in the absolute sense, but the fullness of love that God Himself embodies. It is the goal of the entire chapter: become like your Father.
verses.memory_phrase: Grow to be like your Father in heaven
verses.prayer: God, I want to grow more and more like You — full of love, kindness, and goodness. Help me become like my Father.
verses.discussion_question: What is one way you want to grow to be more like God this week?
verses.family_discussion: How do we keep the call to be 'perfect as God is perfect' from being crushing or discouraging, while still holding it as a genuine goal that shapes daily choices?
verses.do_it_today: Choose one thing from this chapter to practise this week — write it down and put it where you can see it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child looking up into a bright sky, arms open wide, surrounded by a gentle warm golden light that feels like a Father's embrace. Watercolour, soaring and hopeful, full of warmth.
